items.description: loupe-pdf adapter needed per-colorant Lab/CMYK/RGB and a canonical Pantone name on `CodexSpotColorant`; the v1.0.0 schema only carried `name`, `alternate_space_id`, `tint_transform`.
items.originally_flagged_in: QUESTIONABLE-DECISIONS.md 2026-05-07 codex spot-colorant additive Lab/CMYK fields
items.rationale: Added `lab`, `cmyk`, `rgb`, `pantone_name` as optional fields on the existing v1.0.0 model and updated the bundled + split JSON schemas. Defaults `None`; codex-pdf 22/22 tests pass; lint-pdf 3786/3786 tests pass.
evidence: QUESTIONABLE-DECISIONS.md 2026-05-07 codex spot-colorant additive Lab/CMYK fields

items.description: Python extractor (`src/codex_pdf/extract/color.py`) does not yet populate the new Lab/CMYK/RGB/pantone_name fields from PDF Separation tint transforms.
items.originally_flagged_in: QUESTIONABLE-DECISIONS.md 2026-05-07 codex spot-colorant additive Lab/CMYK fields (Risk section)
items.rationale: Schema is additive-ready, but extractor logic to read PDF tint transforms requires a coordinated codex extractor pass. Loupe resolver already falls through to Pantone DB lookup by name, which covers the common case.

items.description: Lint-pdf migrated analyzers needed structured signals (spot/layer names, page-1 operator stream, resource maps) without re-parsing the PDF.
items.originally_flagged_in: QUESTIONABLE-DECISIONS.md 2026-05-07 codex analysis side-channel for dieline parity
items.rationale: Expanded `CodexDocument.analysis` with additive parser-surface signals; lint-pdf rewires through `lintpdf.codex_adapter`. Verified by lint-pdf parity-corpus harness diff_count=0.
evidence: QUESTIONABLE-DECISIONS.md 2026-05-07 codex analysis side-channel for dieline parity

items.description: Cutover criterion 2 required `codex-pdf` golden-master regression suite green.
items.rationale: `uv run pytest tests/test_golden_corpus.py tests/test_parity.py` → 9 passed; `uv run codex-pdf parity ... --profile {summary,inventory,deep} --fail-on-diff` → diff_count: 0 each.
